root_text.an5.111:1.1: “Pañcahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato kulūpako bhikkhu kulesu appiyo ca hoti amanāpo ca agaru ca abhāvanīyo ca.
root_text.an5.111:1.3: Asanthavavissāsī ca hoti, anissaravikappī ca, vissaṭṭhupasevī ca, upakaṇṇakajappī ca, atiyācanako ca.
root_text.an5.111:1.4: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato kulūpako bhikkhu kulesu appiyo ca hoti amanāpo ca agaru ca abhāvanīyo ca.
root_text.an5.111:2.1: Pañcahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato kulūpako bhikkhu kulesu piyo ca hoti manāpo ca garu ca bhāvanīyo ca.
root_text.an5.111:2.3: Na asanthavavissāsī ca hoti, na anissaravikappī ca, na vissaṭṭhupasevī ca, na upakaṇṇakajappī ca, na atiyācanako ca.
root_text.an5.111:2.4: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato kulūpako bhikkhu kulesu piyo ca hoti manāpo ca garu ca bhāvanīyo cā”ti.

translation_text.an5.111:1.1: “Mendicants, a mendicant with five qualities who visits families is unlikable and disagreeable, not respected or admired.
translation_text.an5.111:1.3: They act as though they’re close to people they hardly know. They give away things they don’t own. They over-associate with close friends. They whisper in the ear. And they ask for too much.
translation_text.an5.111:1.4: A mendicant with these five qualities who visits families is unlikable and disagreeable, not respected or admired.
translation_text.an5.111:2.1: A mendicant with five qualities who visits families is dear and beloved, respected and admired.
translation_text.an5.111:2.3: They don’t act as though they’re close to people they hardly know. They don’t give away things they don’t own. They don’t over-associate with close friends. They don’t whisper in the ear. And they don’t ask for too much.
translation_text.an5.111:2.4: A mendicant with these five qualities who visits families is dear and beloved, respected and admired.”

variant_text.an5.111:1.3: Asanthavavissāsī → asanthutavissāsī (bj); asandhavavissāsī (mr) | vissaṭṭhupasevī → viyatthupasevī (bj); byatthupasevī (sya-all, km); vyattūpasevī (pts1ed)
